pages.title: About BelgradeBest
pages.lede: What this site is, who it's for, and the editorial standards every page is held to.
pages.body:
BelgradeBest is an independent, English-language guide to Belgrade, written for foreign visitors, newcomers, and anyone trying to understand the city from the outside — travellers planning a trip, people weighing a longer stay, and those researching medical travel or Expo 2027.

## How every page is researched

Every page is built on a curated, source-checked knowledge base rather than rewritten from other blogs. The process, in plain terms:

- **Deep research first.** Facts are gathered from primary sources — official announcements, institutional data, city and transport authorities, and current on-the-ground reporting — using modern deep-research tools, then compiled into a per-topic knowledge base.
- **Validated, then manually reviewed.** Drafts are checked against that knowledge base, and every page is manually reviewed and revised by the site's editors before it is published. Nothing goes live unread.
- **Confirmed, reported, or unknown.** Where a fact is official, we state it plainly; where it is only reported, we say so; and where something isn't settled yet, we mark it as unknown rather than guess. You'll see this labelling across the Expo 2027 pages especially.
- **Evergreen by design.** We focus on what stays true — how the city works, what's worth your time, how to get around — and leave perishable details, like live prices and exact opening hours, to the official and booking sites that keep them current.
- **Dated updates.** Every article shows when it was last updated, and the date changes only when the page is actually re-checked or revised — not on a timer.

## Corrections

If we got something wrong, we want to know. Tell us the page and what's wrong via the [contact page](/contact) and we'll check it against sources and fix it. We're independent, and we'd rather be corrected than be wrong.

## Medical content

Our medical-tourism pages explain how the sector works and what realistic costs and trade-offs look like. They are **not medical advice**, we never name or endorse specific clinics, and we tell you throughout to verify pricing, credentials and aftercare directly with any provider you consider. Anything clinical belongs between you and a qualified doctor.

## Independence

We are not sponsored by any hotel, clinic, tour operator, or tourism board, and no one pays to be recommended. When we point you toward something to book, it's because it's the genuinely useful answer on the page — see [how we make money](/how-we-make-money) for the full disclosure.

## Who it's for

If you want a polished postcard, there are prettier cities. If you want to actually understand Belgrade and use it well, that's what this guide is for.
pages.seoDescription: How BelgradeBest is researched: primary sources, manual editorial review, confirmed-vs-unknown labelling, dated updates, and a real corrections policy.

pages.title: How we make money
pages.lede: What we get paid for, how we choose what to recommend, and what we will not do.
pages.body:
BelgradeBest is free to read. We keep it that way with lightweight, clearly-marked affiliate links — and nothing else.

## What that means

Some links to booking sites (for example, accommodation) are affiliate links. If you book through one, we may earn a small commission **at no extra cost to you** — you pay the same price you would have anyway. We add these links only where the link is the genuinely useful answer on the page, never wall-to-wall, and we don't run display-ad clutter.

## What we don't do

We don't take payment to recommend a specific hotel, clinic, restaurant, or tour, and we don't let advertisers influence our editorial judgement. A place is mentioned because it earns its place, not because someone paid for it.

## Why it's set up this way

The whole value of this guide is trust. Monetisation is added lightly and visibly, onto pages that are genuinely useful first — never at the expense of honest advice. If a recommendation ever reads like an ad to you, [tell us](/contact).
pages.seoDescription: Honest, plain-English affiliate disclosure for BelgradeBest: marked affiliate links at no extra cost to you, and no paid placements.

pages.title: Get in touch
pages.lede: Tips, corrections, partnership questions. We read everything.
pages.body:
Tips, corrections, partnership questions — we read everything.

Email us at **admin@out25.com**.

Found a factual error or something out of date? That's the most useful message you can send: tell us the page and what's wrong, and we'll fix it. We're independent, and we'd rather be corrected than be wrong.
pages.seoDescription: Get in touch with BelgradeBest — tips, corrections, and partnership questions.

pages.title: Privacy at BelgradeBest
pages.lede: The plain-English version: what we collect, what we don't, and how to reach us about it.
pages.body:
The short, plain-English version.

## What we collect

We use Google Analytics 4 to understand, in aggregate, how people use the site — which pages are read, roughly where visitors come from, and what device they use. This is anonymised, aggregate usage data. We do not collect names, accounts, or payment details, because the site has none. Analytics uses cookies; you can block them in your browser without losing access to anything here.

## Affiliate links

Some outbound booking links are affiliate links (see [how we make money](/how-we-make-money)). If you click one, the destination site may set its own cookies under its own privacy policy, which we don't control.

## Your choices

You can use the whole site with cookies blocked. If you have any question about data, [contact us](/contact). This notice may be updated as the site grows.
pages.seoDescription: How BelgradeBest handles your data — short, plain, and current.
